Harborstone HealthBank, Inc. (US issuer; Pioneer Stock Market “PSM”) — DSAR and Retention Workflow Notice (CCPA/HIPAA/GLBA; Reg FD-aligned processing)
Primary contact: privacy@harborstonehb.example; Investor Relations safe channel: ir@harborstonehb.example; 1‑555‑014‑7724
Data subject: Mr. Rowan Elgin (employee and patient) requests access, deletion, and restriction of: (a) biometric time‑clock face geometry scans; (b) genetic carrier screen result “CF‑carrier positive (screen‑only)”; (c) treatment code T‑47 physical therapy sessions; (d) self‑disclosed religious affiliation “Seventh City Fellowship”; (e) union membership “Allied Care Technicians Local 12.”
Locator and obfuscated IDs: A c c o u n t  I D : HHB‑A9G‑3 9 9; HRIS: HR‑ELGIN‑0x7B; patient chart r e f : CHP/ELGIN/0x3D; portal: https://portal.harborstonehb.example/dsar?case=R‑ELGIN‑2026&tok=pct%2Fus‑ccpa
Scope: Under CCPA we treat consumer/household data reasonably linkable to Mr. Elgin; under HIPAA the above clinical items are PHI; under GLBA any linked application/loan telemetry is NPI and handled under 16 CFR 314.4.
MNPI/Reg FD gate: selective disclosure to covered market actors (sell‑side analysts, buy‑side PMs) is prohibited; DSAR fulfillment will exclude unreleased metrics, forecasts, or non‑public pipeline details and will use only public 8‑K/press materials for context.
No material non‑public financial figures are contained in this packet and we will not produce drafts, “whisper numbers,” or segment margin bridges to Mr. Elgin or any third party.
Retention controls: biometric logs (time‑clock) retained 3 years from last use; genetic screening documentation retained 10 years per clinical policy; HRIS religion/union fields retained 1 year post employment termination; financial system artifacts retained per GLBA schedule (5–7 years).
Transfers/subprocessors: limited processing by CloudPine Services LLC (BAA and GLBA‑aligned DPA on file); data hosted in US regions only; no onward transfer to advertising tech; SFTP to treating provider Dr. Sila Mora upon authenticated request.
Security snapshot: TLS 1.3 in transit; at rest AES‑256; voiceprint hash (v0, non‑reversible) vpr:a93f::0d//b1‑elgin‑red.; we do not store MAC addresses or full device serials.
Benign context: the Northport Mariners season schedule saved in his work calendar is out of scope for DSAR and has no PHI or NPI relevance.
Generic DEI policy text: our Open Doors DEI statement welcomes all sexual orientations; this sentence does not describe any person’s sex‑life or beliefs.
Public‑source cross‑checks (not MNPI): FDA recall bulletins and published payer fee schedules may be referenced for coding validation only.
Internal workflow (lowercase): next steps — route to infosec; legal to review by friday; update jira hb‑privacy sprint; do not email sell‑side analysts directly; escalate any ambiguity to compliance.
Verification: two‑factor via email + SMS OTP; government identifier images, if any, must be redacted (e.g., S SN shown as xx‑xx‑5Q20) before storage; attachments may include O C R a r t i f a c t s.
Submit/track: https://portal.harborstonehb.example/track?u=mr%2Felgin&ref=HHB‑A9G‑399&view=us%2Fpatchwork and privacy hotline voicemail code 7‑F‑3‑A.
Escalation: if any disclosure risk under Rule 10b‑5/Reg FD is suspected, notify Compliance (Ms. Dana Keats, CCO) at compliance@harborstonehb.example within 1 business day.
